We need answer only TS code. Need craft complete TypeScript program Node. Input one line expression. Implement parser/shunting-yard. Need no eval/Function/vm/external. Division truncates toward zero. JS Math.trunc okay. Safe integers. Unary +/-. Precedence: unary +/-, then * /, then + -. Parentheses nested. Need handle input spaces. Complete program.

Need think about ambiguity: expression containing integers, spaces, parentheses, binary + - * /, unary + / -. Need parse. Operators precedence: unary +/- highest. "-1/2" trunc -0? division trunc toward zero => -0 integer 0. JS -1/2 = -0.5 trunc 0. Need output integer. If expression "5/-2" -2.5 trunc -2. Math.trunc(-2.5) -2. Good. For negative division, JS trunc toward zero.

Need complete TypeScript program Node. Could implement recursive descent. Need "real parser or shunting-yard evaluator". Recursive descent accepted. Need read stdin with fs.readFileSync(0, "utf8"). Use process.stdout.write(String(result)).

Need decide parse grammar:
expression := additive
additive := multiplicative ((+|-) multiplicative)*
multiplicative := unary ((*|/) unary)*
unary := (+|-) unary | primary
primary := integer | '(' expression ')'
This makes unary -2 * 3 = (-2)*3. In expression "-1 / 2" parse (-1)/2. Good. But subtle: "-2^2" no. "2 * -3" parse 2* -3 = -6. "2 / -3" -0? 2/-3 = -0.666 trunc 0.

Need handle unary plus. Integers maybe negative? Input integers maybe positive? But unary negative. Lexing numbers. Could scan char by char.

Potential ambiguity: unary vs binary due to context. Recursive descent handles. Need skip spaces.

Need output evaluated integer. All safe.

Could implement scanner with index and peek, next char. Need ensure not treat '-' as unary when not binary. At top of expression, if + or - and previous token is operator or '('? But recursive descent easier: unary at start of multiplicative. Multiplicative left-to-right. For "-2 + 3": parse unary -2 then +. For "1 - -2": after binary -, parse multiplicative at index '-' -> unary -2. Good. For "1 - (2)" binary right primary. For "1 - - (2)" unary.

Need support nested parentheses deeply. Recursive descent can handle deep maybe stack overflow for very deep? "Parentheses may be nested deeply" but no bound. Should we implement iterative shunting-yard to avoid recursion? They specifically allow real parser. Deep nested maybe could exceed JS call stack. Need consider. We can implement shunting-yard evaluator iterative with stacks, handles arbitrarily deep without call stack. But unary precedence tricky. Could implement Pratt parser iteratively? Recursive simple but deep issue. Requirement "may be nested deeply" may expect avoid recursion? Need robust.

Let's think of implementing a real parser or shunting-yard evaluator. We can implement an iterative expression parser / shunting-yard. Need correct unary precedence. Or implement recursive descent with try/catch? Deep maybe not.

Need produce code only. Let's explore options.

Option A: recursive descent. Simpler. But "Parentheses may be nested deeply" perhaps test deep 100k? Node call stack may fail. Requirement may be to support deep. Better implement iterative parser/shunting-yard.

Shunting-yard algorithm:
Input tokens: numbers, operators (+ - * /), parentheses. Convert to RPN or evaluate directly.

Unary +/-. Need handle highest precedence. Unary operators bind to following operand. In shunting-yard, unary operator can be placed on operator stack with precedence 3, right-associative, arity 1. Need output unary marker. Then evaluate stack: for unary op, pop operand and apply. Need distinguish unary from binary.

Algorithm:
while tokens:
if number push
if op + - * /:
while top op has greater precedence than current or equal and current not right-assoc:
pop op apply
push current
if '(':
push
if ')':
pop until '(' apply; pop '('
At end pop all apply.

Need precedence: unary 3, * / 2, + - 1. Right-assoc for unary and parentheses. Need apply unary before *? Let's test.

Tokens: 1 - -2. Output? Start 1 push. '-' binary push. next '-' op with prec3. while top op '-' prec1 >3 false. push '-' (unary). Next 2 push. End pop unary -> -2; pop binary - -> 3. Good.

Tokens: -2 * 3. Start '-' push. 2 push. '*' op prec2. while top '-' prec3 >2 true pop unary -> -2. push '*' push 3. End pop '*' -> -6. Good. This means unary before multiplication. Good.

Tokens: 2 * -3. After 2 * push, '-' prec3. while top * prec2 >3 false push unary. 3 push. End pop unary -> -3; pop * -> -6. Good.

Tokens: -2 / 3 -> -0? yes.

Tokens: 1 + -2 * 3. Start 1 + push. '-' prec3 top + prec1 no push. 2 push. * prec2. while top '-' prec3 >2 pop unary -2. push *. 3 push. End pop * (-6), pop + => -5. Good.

Tokens: -(2+3) => '-' push, '(' push, 2 push, + push, 3 push, ')' pop +, pop '(', end pop '-' unary -> -5. Good.

Tokens: 2 * -(3+4) => after 2 * push; '-' push; '(' push; 3 + 4; ')' pop +, pop '('; end pop '-' unary -7; pop * -14. Good.

Tokens: 2^-? no.

Tokens: 5 / -2 => 5 / unary -2 = -2.

Tokens: - - 2 => --2. '-' push, '-' push, 2, end pop unary 2, pop unary -2. Good.

Need handle unary +.

Could implement scanner char-by-char:
function scan(input: string): Token[] {
const tokens: Token[] = [];
let i=0, n=input.length;
while(i<n){
const ch=input[i];
if(/\s/.test(ch)){ i++; continue; }
if(isDigit(ch)){
let j=i+1;
while(j<n && isDigit(input[j])) j++;
tokens.push({type:"number", value:Number(input.slice(i,j))});
i=j;
} else if(ch === '(' || ch===')') { tokens.push({type:"paren", value:ch}); i++; }
else if(ch==='+'||ch==='-'||ch==='*'||ch==='/') { tokens.push({type:"op", value:ch}); i++; }
else throw new Error("Unexpected character");
}
return tokens;
}

Function precedence:
function isBinary(op: string): op is "+"|"-"|"*"|"/" { return ...}
function opPrec(op:string): number { if op==='+'||op==='-' return 1; if op==='*'||op==='/' return 2; return 3; } // includes unary.
function isUnaryOp(op: string): boolean { return op === "+" || op === "-"; }
function isRightAssociative(op: string): boolean { return op === "+" || op === "-" || op === "(": hmm for '('? When current op is '(', right-assoc true. In shunting-yard, if current op is '(', push regardless. For current op '*' right-assoc false. For current op '-' could be unary or binary but right-assoc true. Good.
function shouldPop(currentOp, topOp):
if topOp === '(' false;
if !isBinary(currentOp) return false; // current op op only
if currentOp === "(" return false;
const pCurrent = precedence(currentOp);
const pTop = precedence(topOp);
return pTop > pCurrent || (pTop === pCurrent && !isRightAssociative(currentOp));

For current op '*' pCurrent 2, top unary '-' pTop 1? Wait unary '-' precedence 3. Good.
For current op '+' pCurrent 1, top '+' pTop1, right assoc true don't pop. Good.

return topPrec > currentPrec || (topPrec === currentPrec && !rightAssociative(current));

const operand = values.pop();

return op === "-" ? -operand : operand;
}
const right = values.pop();

if undefined error
switch...
}
